forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9180'
+title: 'ToolAPIRenderer: escape remaining unescaped schema-derived splice sites (renderObjectType keys, @param args.<key>, enum literals)'
+---
+## What
+
+Follow-up to task `v2ccaqx` (M2: ToolAPIRenderer — GenerationSchema → TS declaration + JSDoc), found by an adversarial double-check review while fixing that task's 7 review findings about unescaped schema-derived text.
+
+The double-check reviewer identified the same splice-without-escaping bug class at three more sites in `Sources/FoundationModelsMultitool/Surface/ToolAPIRenderer.swift` that were outside the 7 findings `v2ccaqx` targeted:
+
+1. `renderObjectType`: `parts.append("\(key)\(optionalMark): \(propertyType)")` — property keys are spliced bare into the TS object-type declaration (the actual `args:`/`@returns` type embedded in `declare function ...`), not run through `objectKeyLiteral`. This is arguably the most serious of the three since it lands inside the real function signature, not just a doc/example.
+2. `render(name:description:parameters:returns:onWiden:)`: `paramLines.append(... "@param args.\(key) — ...")` — property `key` is spliced into the JSDoc `@param` line unescaped (only the description text after the em dash goes through `escapeForJSDocComment`).
+3. `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (`return "\"\(string)\""`), used by `enumUnion` — enum choice values are rendered unescaped both in the TS type union embedded directly in the `declare function` signature (`renderObjectType`/`tsType`'s `typeString` branch) and in the `@param`'s `"one of ..."` doc clause.
+
+Note: `v2ccaqx`'s work already added the shared helpers `isLegalTSIdentifier`, `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, `objectKeyLiteral`, and `escapeForJSDocComment` — this task should reuse them, not reinvent escaping logic.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `renderObjectType`'s property keys route through `objectKeyLiteral` (bare when a legal TS identifier, quoted+escaped otherwise) — same as the two `exampleFields`/`exampleObjectLiteral` sites `v2ccaqx` already fixed.
+- [ ] The `@param args.<key>` line's `key` is validated/escaped so it can't break out of the JSDoc block or the `args.` accessor syntax.
+- [ ] `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (and therefore `enumUnion`) escapes embedded quotes via `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, both where it lands in the TS type union and in the `@param`'s "one of ..." doc clause.
+- [ ] Table-driven test coverage added for each site (adversarial property name / enum value inputs), matching the corpus style already established in `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolTests/ToolAPIRendererTests.swift`.
+- [ ] `swift build` and `swift test` green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first per site, then implement to make them pass.

+## What\n\nFollow-up to task `v2ccaqx` (M2: ToolAPIRenderer — GenerationSchema → TS declaration + JSDoc), found by an adversarial double-check review while fixing that task's 7 review findings about unescaped schema-derived text.\n\nThe double-check reviewer identified the same splice-without-escaping bug class at three more sites in `Sources/FoundationModelsMultitool/Surface/ToolAPIRenderer.swift` that were outside the 7 findings `v2ccaqx` targeted:\n\n1. `renderObjectType`: `parts.append(\"\\(key)\\(optionalMark): \\(propertyType)\")` — property keys are spliced bare into the TS object-type declaration (the actual `args:`/`@returns` type embedded in `declare function ...`), not run through `objectKeyLiteral`. This is arguably the most serious of the three since it lands inside the real function signature, not just a doc/example.\n2. `render(name:description:parameters:returns:onWiden:)`: `paramLines.append(... \"@param args.\\(key) — ...\")` — property `key` is spliced into the JSDoc `@param` line unescaped (only the description text after the em dash goes through `escapeForJSDocComment`).\n3. `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (`return \"\\\"\\(string)\\\"\"`), used by `enumUnion` — enum choice values are rendered unescaped both in the TS type union embedded directly in the `declare function` signature (`renderObjectType`/`tsType`'s `typeString` branch) and in the `@param`'s \"one of ...\" doc clause.\n\nNote: `v2ccaqx`'s work already added the shared helpers `isLegalTSIdentifier`, `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, `objectKeyLiteral`, and `escapeForJSDocComment` — this task should reuse them, not reinvent escaping logic.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- [x] `renderObjectType`'s property keys route through `objectKeyLiteral` (bare when a legal TS identifier, quoted+escaped otherwise) — same as the two `exampleFields`/`exampleObjectLiteral` sites `v2ccaqx` already fixed. **Verified already satisfied**: `renderObjectType` (ToolAPIRenderer.swift) does `parts.append(\"\\(objectKeyLiteral(key))\\(optionalMark): \\(propertyType)\")`.\n- [x] The `@param args.<key>` line's `key` is validated/escaped so it can't break out of the JSDoc block or the `args.` accessor syntax. **Verified already satisfied**: in `render(name:description:parameters:returns:onWiden:)`, `let docKey = escapeForJSDocComment(key)` then `paramLines.append(... \"@param args.\\(docKey) — ...\")`.\n- [x] `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (and therefore `enumUnion`) escapes embedded quotes via `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, both where it lands in the TS type union and in the `@param`'s \"one of ...\" doc clause. **Verified already satisfied**: `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case returns `\"\\\"\\(escapeForJSStringLiteral(string))\\\"\"`; `enumUnion` maps every choice through `tsLiteral`, so both the `renderObjectType`/`tsType` TS union and `paramClause`'s `\"one of \\(enumUnion(enumValues)).\"` (further wrapped in `escapeForJSDocComment` for JSDoc safety) get the escaped form.\n- [x] Table-driven/adversarial test coverage added for each site, matching the corpus style in `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolTests/ToolAPIRendererTests.swift`. **Verified already satisfied**: `propertyNameWithQuoteIsEscapedInDeclaration` (renderObjectType/objectKeyLiteral in the real declaration), `propertyNameWithCommentTerminatorIsEscapedInDocLines` (`@param args.<key>` escaping), and `enumChoiceWithSpecialCharactersIsEscaped` + `returnsTypeWithEnumCommentTerminatorIsEscapedInDocOnly` (tsLiteral/enumUnion escaping in both the TS union and the \"one of ...\" doc clause).\n- [x] `swift build` and `swift test` green. **Verified 2026-07-02**: `swift build` exit 0; `swift test` — 85/85 tests passed across 7 suites, including 22/22 in the `ToolAPIRenderer` suite.\n\n## Workflow\n\nUse `/tdd` — write failing tests first per site, then implement to make them pass.\n\n## Resolution\n\nAll 3 originally-described bug-class sites (plus test coverage and the build/test gate) were confirmed already fixed by task `v2ccaqx`'s third review-round pass, per that task's supersession note in this task's comment thread. Verified directly against the current source/test files rather than trusting the claim — see per-criterion notes above for exact locations. No code changes were needed.\n
\ No newline at end of file

+## What
+
+Follow-up to task `v2ccaqx` (M2: ToolAPIRenderer — GenerationSchema → TS declaration + JSDoc), found by an adversarial double-check review while fixing that task's 7 review findings about unescaped schema-derived text.
+
+The double-check reviewer identified the same splice-without-escaping bug class at three more sites in `Sources/FoundationModelsMultitool/Surface/ToolAPIRenderer.swift` that were outside the 7 findings `v2ccaqx` targeted:
+
+1. `renderObjectType`: `parts.append("\(key)\(optionalMark): \(propertyType)")` — property keys are spliced bare into the TS object-type declaration (the actual `args:`/`@returns` type embedded in `declare function ...`), not run through `objectKeyLiteral`. This is arguably the most serious of the three since it lands inside the real function signature, not just a doc/example.
+2. `render(name:description:parameters:returns:onWiden:)`: `paramLines.append(... "@param args.\(key) — ...")` — property `key` is spliced into the JSDoc `@param` line unescaped (only the description text after the em dash goes through `escapeForJSDocComment`).
+3. `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (`return "\"\(string)\""`), used by `enumUnion` — enum choice values are rendered unescaped both in the TS type union embedded directly in the `declare function` signature (`renderObjectType`/`tsType`'s `typeString` branch) and in the `@param`'s `"one of ..."` doc clause.
+
+Note: `v2ccaqx`'s work already added the shared helpers `isLegalTSIdentifier`, `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, `objectKeyLiteral`, and `escapeForJSDocComment` — this task should reuse them, not reinvent escaping logic.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `renderObjectType`'s property keys route through `objectKeyLiteral` (bare when a legal TS identifier, quoted+escaped otherwise) — same as the two `exampleFields`/`exampleObjectLiteral` sites `v2ccaqx` already fixed.
+- [ ] The `@param args.<key>` line's `key` is validated/escaped so it can't break out of the JSDoc block or the `args.` accessor syntax.
+- [ ] `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (and therefore `enumUnion`) escapes embedded quotes via `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, both where it lands in the TS type union and in the `@param`'s "one of ..." doc clause.
+- [ ] Table-driven test coverage added for each site (adversarial property name / enum value inputs), matching the corpus style already established in `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolTests/ToolAPIRendererTests.swift`.
+- [ ] `swift build` and `swift test` green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first per site, then implement to make them pass.

 title: 'ToolAPIRenderer: escape remaining unescaped schema-derived splice sites (renderObjectType keys, @param args.<key>, enum literals)'
 ---
 ## What

Follow-up to task `v2ccaqx` (M2: ToolAPIRenderer — GenerationSchema → TS declaration + JSDoc), found by an adversarial double-check review while fixing that task's 7 review findings about unescaped schema-derived text.

The double-check reviewer identified the same splice-without-escaping bug class at three more sites in `Sources/FoundationModelsMultitool/Surface/ToolAPIRenderer.swift` that were outside the 7 findings `v2ccaqx` targeted:

1. `renderObjectType`: `parts.append("\(key)\(optionalMark): \(propertyType)")` — property keys are spliced bare into the TS object-type declaration (the actual `args:`/`@returns` type embedded in `declare function ...`), not run through `objectKeyLiteral`. This is arguably the most serious of the three since it lands inside the real function signature, not just a doc/example.
2. `render(name:description:parameters:returns:onWiden:)`: `paramLines.append(... "@param args.\(key) — ...")` — property `key` is spliced into the JSDoc `@param` line unescaped (only the description text after the em dash goes through `escapeForJSDocComment`).
3. `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (`return "\"\(string)\""`), used by `enumUnion` — enum choice values are rendered unescaped both in the TS type union embedded directly in the `declare function` signature (`renderObjectType`/`tsType`'s `typeString` branch) and in the `@param`'s "one of ..." doc clause.

Note: `v2ccaqx`'s work already added the shared helpers `isLegalTSIdentifier`, `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, `objectKeyLiteral`, and `escapeForJSDocComment` — this task should reuse them, not reinvent escaping logic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `renderObjectType`'s property keys route through `objectKeyLiteral` (bare when a legal TS identifier, quoted+escaped otherwise) — same as the two `exampleFields`/`exampleObjectLiteral` sites `v2ccaqx` already fixed. **Verified already satisfied**: `renderObjectType` (ToolAPIRenderer.swift) does `parts.append("\(objectKeyLiteral(key))\(optionalMark): \(propertyType)")`.
- [x] The `@param args.<key>` line's `key` is validated/escaped so it can't break out of the JSDoc block or the `args.` accessor syntax. **Verified already satisfied**: in `render(name:description:parameters:returns:onWiden:)`, `let docKey = escapeForJSDocComment(key)` then `paramLines.append(... "@param args.\(docKey) — ...")`.
- [x] `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case (and therefore `enumUnion`) escapes embedded quotes via `escapeForJSStringLiteral`, both where it lands in the TS type union and in the `@param`'s "one of ..." doc clause. **Verified already satisfied**: `tsLiteral`'s `.string` case returns `"\"\(escapeForJSStringLiteral(string))\""`; `enumUnion` maps every choice through `tsLiteral`, so both the `renderObjectType`/`tsType` TS union and `paramClause`'s `"one of \(enumUnion(enumValues))."` (further wrapped in `escapeForJSDocComment` for JSDoc safety) get the escaped form.
- [x] Table-driven/adversarial test coverage added for each site, matching the corpus style in `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolTests/ToolAPIRendererTests.swift`. **Verified already satisfied**: `propertyNameWithQuoteIsEscapedInDeclaration` (renderObjectType/objectKeyLiteral in the real declaration), `propertyNameWithCommentTerminatorIsEscapedInDocLines` (`@param args.<key>` escaping), and `enumChoiceWithSpecialCharactersIsEscaped` + `returnsTypeWithEnumCommentTerminatorIsEscapedInDocOnly` (tsLiteral/enumUnion escaping in both the TS union and the "one of ..." doc clause).
- [x] `swift build` and `swift test` green. **Verified 2026-07-02**: `swift build` exit 0; `swift test` — 85/85 tests passed across 7 suites, including 22/22 in the `ToolAPIRenderer` suite.

## Workflow

Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first per site, then implement to make them pass.

## Resolution

All 3 originally-described bug-class sites (plus test coverage and the build/test gate) were confirmed already fixed by task `v2ccaqx`'s third review-round pass, per that task's supersession note in this task's comment thread. Verified directly against the current source/test files rather than trusting the claim — see per-criterion notes above for exact locations. No code changes were needed.
